subtasks.description: Query vendor reliability data including incident counts, uptime metrics, and historical performance scores from the vendor database
Reconcile vendor reliability metrics with incident history and classify each vendor into low_risk, medium_risk, or high_risk categories based on incident patterns and performance thresholds

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks: (1) sql_gen at small tier to fetch vendor reliability metrics from a single SQL source—straightforward relational query; (2) cross_recon at mid tier to apply classification logic across the vendor dataset, assigning risk categories based on incident history and performance thresholds. cross_recon is appropriate here because it consolidates metrics from the query result and applies rule-based classification as part of reconciliation, not as a separate policy_action (which would only apply if a governance policy decision were required). Mid tier is sufficient because the classification involves multi-signal reasoning (incidents + reliability) but not complex cross-source reconciliation.

output.sql: SELECT vendor_name, COUNT(invoice_id) as incident_count, COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'paid' THEN 1 END) as successful_payments, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'paid' THEN 1 END) / COUNT(invoice_id), 2) as uptime_percentage, CASE WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN status != 'paid' THEN 1 END) <= 2 THEN 'low_risk' WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN status != 'paid' THEN 1 END) BETWEEN 3 AND 5 THEN 'medium_risk' ELSE 'high_risk' END as vendor_risk_classification FROM invoice_facts GROUP BY vendor_name ORDER BY incident_count DESC

output.raw_output:
